title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy eyes are infusions made from dried herbs that can help alleviate the discomfort and irritation caused by itchy eyes. These teas contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness. Chamomile and calendula are two popular herbs used in herbal teas for itchy eyes. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating eye irritations caused by allergies or infections. Calendula, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antifungal properties that can help soothe and heal irritated eyes. By using these herbal teas, individuals can experience relief from itchy eyes, leading to improved sleep, increased productivity, and overall better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The tea's flavonoids and anthocyanins help to reduce redness and swelling, while its astringent properties constrict blood vessels, decreasing the release of histamine that can exacerbate itchiness. Additionally, eyebright tea's antiseptic qualities help to eliminate any underlying infection or allergen that may be causing the itchiness, providing fast and effective relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, relieving itchiness and discomfort.

Antihistamine-like effects: The bioactive compounds in Eyebright tea, including alkaloids and glycosides, have antihistamine-like properties that help block histamine receptors, reducing itching, redness, and excess tearing associated with allergies or irritation.

Soften and lubricate the eyes: Eyebright tea's mucilages and saponins create a soothing, protective film on the surface of the eye, softening and lubricating the eyes, which helps to alleviate itchiness, dryness, and discomfort caused by conditions like dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes, providing relief from irritation.
Leaves: Eyebright leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eyebright leaves and flowers for optimal flavor and potency ensuring at least an ounce of dried material per cup.
Combine the dried eyebright with boiling water in a teapot allowing it to steep for exactly five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth removing any solids and residue.
Add honey or lemon to taste as desired and sweeten to preference using one to two teaspoons of honey.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature within thirty minutes of preparation for maximum benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The plantain leaf contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce swelling and relieve itching caused by allergies or dryness. When brewed into a tea, the active compounds are absorbed into the eye tissue, providing quick relief from itchy, red, and watery eyes. This natural remedy offers an effective and gentle solution for alleviating itchiness and promoting healthy eye function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea reduces inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which helps to alleviate itchiness and discomfort caused by allergic reactions or irritants.

Antihistamine properties: The antihistamine compounds in plantain tea help to neutralize histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that can cause itching, redness, and irritation in the eyes.

Astringent properties: Plantain tea has astringent compounds that help to reduce discharge and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the eyes, which can provide relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the plantain plant have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves, choose healthy and vibrant ones for best quality.
Rinse the harvested leaves with clean water, then pat them dry gently with a soft cloth.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried plantain leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves with 8 oz boiling water in a teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on personal preference for tea strength and flavor.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discard solids, and serve hot or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic compounds work to calm irritated ocular tissues, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the apigenin present in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and easing stress-related eye issues, which can often exacerbate itchiness. As a result, drinking German chamomile tea can provide quick relief from itchy eyes, leaving you feeling more comfortable and relaxed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eyes and other eye problems.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm and soothe irritated eyes and reduce itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: German chamomile roots are rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the German chamomile plant contain azulene, a natural antihistamine that helps to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried german chamomile flowers for every eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water in a tea kettle or pot to boiling point for five minutes.
Remove the water from heat and add the measured german chamomile flowers for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep for ten to fifteen minutes while it cools down slightly.
Strain the tea into cups or bottles using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated conjunctiva. The tea's flavonoids and carotenoids work together to reduce redness and swelling, providing quick relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dry air, or other environmental irritants. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate eye tissues from further damage, promoting a healthy and comfortable visual experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and redness associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which contributes to itchiness and other eye problems.
Calming and soothing property: Pot marigold tea has a calming effect on the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by allergies, dryness, or other conditions that can lead to itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and saponins that provide antihistamine and anti-allergic properties, relieving itchy eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers for optimal flavor and potency.
Rinse the dried or fresh flowers with cold running water to remove any impurities.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh calendula petals with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse and release their flavors and benefits.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyelids. The tea's antioxidant compounds also help to reduce redness and swelling, providing quick relief from itchiness. Additionally, comfrey's natural antimicrobial agents work to eliminate any underlying infections or allergies that may be causing the itchiness. By drinking comfrey tea, individuals can experience a significant reduction in eye irritation, allowing them to enjoy clear and comfortable vision once again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of comfrey tea, including compounds like rosmarinic acid, help combat bacterial infections that can cause itchy eyes and promote healing.
Antioxidant: Comfrey's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids, protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory that soothes and calms irritated eyes.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and glycosides that help reduce swelling and inflammation, providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and stems, drying them thoroughly to prevent mold formation.
Measure one teaspoon of dried comfrey per 8 ounces of boiling water, for every serving needed.
Steep the comfrey in hot water for 5-7 minutes, allowing its active compounds to infuse into solution.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and preserving liquid extract.
Allow the comfrey tea to cool, then store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which work to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbs in St John's Wort tea, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to reduce redness and swelling, while also alleviating itchiness and discomfort caused by allergies or irritants. Additionally, the calming effects of St John's Wort can promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can often exacerbate eye issues. As a result, sipping on a cup of St John's Wort tea may provide much-needed relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: St. John's Wort tea can relieve itchy eyes by counteracting histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that causes itching and inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in St. John's Wort tea can reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in St. John's Wort tea help protect the eyes from free radical damage, which can contribute to eye irritation and itchiness, and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with itchy eyes.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried st john's wort flowers at dawn or evening for maximum potency.
Rinse one teaspoon of dried st john's wort flowers or two teaspoons of fresh petals with cold filtered water.
Steep the herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavonoids and essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids immediately.
Enjoy your hot tea after 10-15 minutes of steeping, or store it in an airtight container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenes work to reduce swelling and redness, while also calming irritated skin and mucous membranes. As a natural anti-irritant, yarrow tea soothes and cools the eyes, providing instant relief from itchy, scratchy sensations caused by allergies, dry air, or other environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial agents present in yarrow tea can help combat bacterial or viral infections that may be causing eye irritation and itchiness, promoting healing and soothing the affected area.

Vasoconstrictive property: Yarrow tea's vasoconstrictive properties can help reduce blood flow to the eyes, which can alleviate itching and burning sensations caused by increased blood circulation in response to inflammation or infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itching and discomfort.
Flowers: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, flowers are often used to reduce swelling and redness associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves flowers and stems in moderation to avoid over-harvesting.
Rinse 1-2 teaspoons of chopped yarrow leaves flowers and stems with cold filtered water for 30 seconds.
Combine rinsed yarrow mixture with 8 ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized teapot for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove solids.
Let the tea steep at room temperature for an additional 10-15 minutes before serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling. The silica content in horsetail also soothes and calms the eye tissue, providing relief from irritation and itching. Additionally, horsetail's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye dryness and itchiness, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in horsetail tea neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, reducing itchy sensations.
Soothing demulcent properties: Horsetail's mucilaginous compounds create a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, horsetail roots help to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy eyes by promoting healthy mucous membranes.
Rhyzomes: Acting as a natural anti-inflammatory, horsetail rhyzomes calm irritated eyes and provide relief from itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried horsetail plant material equisetum arvense in abundance.
Rinse the harvested horsetail with cold running water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Measure one teaspoon of dried horsetail or two tablespoons of fresh horsetail into a teapot generously.
Bring one cup of boiling water to a boil and pour over the horsetail allowing it steep for five minutes slowly.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids before serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blackcurrant teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The antioxidants present in blackcurrants also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness caused by allergies, dryness, or other environmental factors. Additionally, the flavonoids found in blackcurrant tea have a natural ability to relax blood vessels, reducing redness and puffiness associated with itchy eyes, providing long-lasting relief for itchy and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blackcurrant tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to itchy eyes and other eye irritation.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Blackcurrant contains flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and itchiness.

Vitamins and minerals: Blackcurrant tea is rich in vitamins C and K, as well as potassium and other essential minerals, which can help to promote healthy ocular function, reduce fatigue, and alleviate dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids, they provide a natural anti-inflammatory effect that alleviates itchiness and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried blackcurrants ribes nigrum at peak ripeness for optimal flavor potency.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried blackcurrant leaves or one tablespoon of fresh blackcurrant berries per cup of water.
Heat filtered water to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Steep the blackcurrants in the hot water for an additional 10-15 minutes to release the flavorful compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with itchy eyes because its unique blend of compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These ingredients help to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes, providing fast relief from itchy sensations. As the tea is absorbed into the body, it also helps to relax the muscles around the eyes, further reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of calm. This natural remedy is an effective way to find instant relief from itchy eyes without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to inflammation and itchiness in the eyes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's anti-inflammatory compounds, like ginkgolic acids, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes by inhibiting prostaglandins and other pro-inflammatory mediators, alleviating itchy eye symptoms.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba tea's vasodilatory properties help to dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving blood circulation and reducing congestion, which can contribute to itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Bark: Contains tannins that provide astringent properties, reducing redness and itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water in a teapot or kettle to a rolling boil for three to five minutes.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove the solids.
Enjoy the warm herbal tea immediately, or let it cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy eyes with herbal teas is by using chamomile or fennel tea bags. Steep the tea bags in hot water for several minutes, then let them cool down. Once they're at a comfortable temperature, place the tea bags on closed eyelids for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day. Both chamomile and fennel have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated eyes and reduce itchiness. This natural remedy is a great alternative to over-the-counter medications and can provide instant relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal teas include dry eye, eye strain, and pink eye. Chamomile tea is a popular choice for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing irritated eyes and reducing inflammation. Meanwhile, calendula tea is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a suitable treatment for pink eye and other eye infections. For dry eye, fennel tea can help stimulate tear production and reduce inflammation. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for eye ailments.
